summary: Command injection vulnerability through unsanitized host parameter concatenated into shell command
why_it_matters: An attacker can inject arbitrary shell commands by providing malicious input like '127.0.0.1; rm -rf /' to execute unauthorized commands on the system

summary: Command injection vulnerability through unsanitized filename parameter concatenated into shell command
why_it_matters: An attacker can execute arbitrary commands by injecting shell metacharacters in the filename parameter, potentially leading to complete system compromise

summary: Hard-coded JWT signing secret embedded in source code
why_it_matters: Attackers with source code access can forge valid tokens and impersonate any user

summary: Weak cryptographic secret with insufficient entropy
why_it_matters: The 14-character secret is vulnerable to brute-force attacks allowing token forgery
